Name: adaptive_kv_cache_layout
Title: Adaptive KV-Cache Layout Optimization for Efficient LLM Inference
Short Hypothesis: Dynamic adaptation of the KV-cache layout based on workload characteristics can significantly improve memory-bandwidth utilization and reduce wall-clock latency in LLM inference.
Related Work: Recent works like AMP-ViT and SNIP focus on mixed-precision quantization and adaptive training frameworks but do not address the specific challenges of KV-cache optimization in LLM inference. HiLoRA introduces adaptive hierarchical LoRA routing but does not consider memory layout optimizations. Our proposal uniquely targets KV-cache layout adaptation, leveraging profiled workload characteristics to optimize memory-bandwidth utilization and inference latency.
Abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) often face inefficiencies due to suboptimal memory utilization, particularly in the KV-cache. This paper proposes an adaptive KV-cache layout optimization technique that dynamically adjusts the memory layout based on profiled workload characteristics. By leveraging detailed hardware profiling metrics such as coalesced loads, reduced HBM round-trips, and better register allocation, our approach aims to improve memory-bandwidth utilization and reduce wall-clock latency. We introduce a set of empirically tuned heuristics for adaptive layout selection, validated through A/B latency races under fixed accuracy budgets. Our experiments demonstrate significant improvements in tokens-per-second and energy per token on commodity GPUs, providing a practical, deployable solution for efficient LLM inference.
Experiments: Profile baseline LLM inference to collect metrics on coalesced loads, HBM round-trips, and register allocation.
Develop adaptive KV-cache layout heuristics based on profiled workload characteristics.
Implement the optimized KV-cache layout in CUDA/Triton kernels and measure wall-clock latency, tokens-per-second, and energy per token.
Conduct A/B latency races comparing the baseline and optimized layouts under fixed accuracy budgets.
Evaluate performance across multiple GPUs/driver versions, reporting p50/p99 latency and throughput under batching.
Risk Factors and Limitations: Potential overhead from dynamic layout adaptation may outweigh the benefits in certain workloads.
Optimization effectiveness may vary across different GPU architectures.
Empirical tuning of heuristics may require extensive profiling and iteration.
